title: philotheca cuticularis
intro: Philotheca cuticularis, also known as the Waxflower, is a flowering plant native to Australia, belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Philotheca cuticularis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of Philotheca cuticularis include essential oils and infusions, which can be used topically or internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
